sections.paragraph:
ποθητός, ή, όν (on the formation s. ποθέω, cp. Hom. ποθή; Aelian, NA 7, 3; Alciphron 3, 39, 2; IG VII 3434; Ramsay, Phrygia I/2 p. 386 ln. 3 τέκνα π.; Just., D. 57, 4; Mel., P. 26, 186) longed-for, dearly beloved IRo 10:1; ISm 13:2; IPol 8:3.—DELG s.v. ποθέω.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Ramsay Ramsay = WR.—Lists 3, 6

  1. ln. = line

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 839
